prompt:
How can I extract data from an HTML table and use Ruby to generate a summary of "The Benefits of Volunteering" article based on the Volunteer_Work table with columns Volunteer_ID, Volunteer_Name, Activity_Type, Duration_Hours, and Positive_Effects? Can you provide an example code snippet for this task? Assuming the HTML page looks like this:
Volunteer ID Volunteer Name Activity Type Duration Hours Positive Effects
1 John Teaching 4 Improved student performance
2 Jane Community service 3 Improved community involvement
3 Bob Mentoring 2 Improved self-esteem for mentee
You can use Nokogiri gem to extract data from the table and summarize it using the following code snippet: require 'nokogiri' require 'open-uri' doc = Nokogiri::HTML(open('path/to/html/file.html')) table = doc.css('table') volunteer_data = [] table.css('tr').each do |row| volunteer_data << { id: row.css('td')[0].text, name: row.css('td')[1].text, activity: row.css('td')[2].text, duration: row.css('td')[3].text, effect: row.css('td')[4].text } end summary = "Summary of The Benefits of Volunteering:\n\n" positive_effects = [] volunteer_data.each do |volunteer| positive_effects << volunteer[:effect] end summary << "The positive effects of volunteering include:\n" summary << "- " + positive_effects.join("\n- ") puts summary This code snippet extracts the data from the HTML table and stores it in an array of hashes. It then generates a summary of the positive effects of volunteering by iterating through the array and appending the effects to a string. Finally, it prints out the summary.
